meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup Without Coding (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Learn to implement robust analytics for your SaaS without coding experience. Master Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid, data-driven product growth.
content.answer_block: Bypass complex engineering by using Antigravity to generate event-tracking schemas and Claude Code to inject them into your stack. By mapping user interactions to business KPIs, you gain actionable data in hours, not weeks. This automated approach ensures your MVP remains lean while providing deep insights into user retention.
content.intro:
You’ve launched your MVP, but you’re flying blind. Every user click is a mystery, and your growth strategy is based on gut feeling rather than cold, hard data. For the non-technical founder, the barrier to setting up enterprise-grade analytics feels like a wall of impossible code and documentation.

But here is the reality: your product's success isn't determined by how much code you write, but by how quickly you learn from user behavior. By leveraging AI-assisted workflows, you can bypass the manual instrumentation headache. Today, we turn that 'no-code' barrier into your competitive advantage by deploying a professional-grade analytics architecture in under an hour.

steps.title: Define Your KPI Schema
steps.description: Before touching code, define exactly what user actions translate to revenue. Focus on 'North Star' events like sign-ups, feature usage, and churn indicators.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a SaaS analytics tracking plan. Include events for 'user_signup', 'feature_activation', and 'subscription_upgrade'. Map these to properties like user_id, timestamp, and referral_source.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a tracking_plan.json based on the high-conversion SaaS metrics defined in the Antigravity schema.'

steps.title: Automate Analytics Injection
steps.description: Use Claude Code to identify the entry points in your frontend codebase where events should trigger, ensuring no manual boilerplate is written.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a lightweight wrapper function in JavaScript that sends event data to an analytics endpoint. It should handle batching and error retries automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Inject the analytics wrapper into the main app entry point and hook it into the authentication flow.'

steps.title: Validate Event Streams
steps.description: Verification is critical. We must ensure that the events fired by your application are correctly formatted and received by your analytics provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script to simulate user behavior and verify that the analytics payloads match the schema defined in Step 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the validation script and log any discrepancies between the frontend events and the schema.'

steps.title: Implement Retention Logic
steps.description: Analytics are useless without context. We now tie user IDs to session duration to calculate churn risk.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate code to calculate session duration and send an 'active_session' event every 5 minutes during user activity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement the session heartbeat logic in the dashboard component to track engagement duration.'

steps.title: Dashboard Visualization
steps.description: Automate the creation of a high-level overview dashboard that displays your conversion metrics in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL query snippet to aggregate user signups by week and calculate the conversion rate from trial to paid.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a report configuration file based on the collected event data for the executive dashboard.'

pro_tips: Always track 'Negative Events'—like a user clicking a button and getting an error—to find friction points instantly.
Implement 'Identity Merging' early to ensure your analytics track a user across devices, not just browser sessions.
Use sampling for high-traffic events to keep your analytics costs low while maintaining 95% statistical significance.
Set up automated Slack alerts for when key conversion metrics drop below a 3-day rolling average.
Never hardcode analytics keys; always inject them via environment variables to keep your repository secure.
content.next_steps: Once your events are flowing, schedule a 30-minute review every Monday to analyze the data. Use the findings to iterate on your UI, specifically targeting the steps with the highest drop-off rates.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to scale based on data rather than guessing. Don't let your growth stagnate because you couldn't see the numbers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
